matches.match_rationale: The core pain is mapping data from SIS, LMS, and finance systems into a unified schema — exactly what AI-assisted ETL and schema mapping addresses. Current manual consolidation in Excel is the textbook use case: semi-structured inputs from multiple systems need reshaping and normalization into a target reporting structure. Cross-system reconciliation (billed vs. enrolled) is a direct schema-mapping and data-cleaning problem.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI-assisted ETL layer that automatically maps exported CSVs/reports from each system to a canonical schema, flags reconciliation mismatches, and outputs a unified report-ready dataset — eliminating the 5-7 day manual consolidation effort.

matches.match_rationale: Late discovery of discrepancies is explicitly named as a pain — billed vs. enrolled mismatches, unexpected drops in enrollment, and outlier campus performance are all anomaly-detection problems in structured data. An anomaly model trained on historical cross-system data can surface these discrepancies in near-real-time rather than at month-end reconciliation.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated anomaly detection layer that continuously monitors SIS/finance data streams for enrollment-billing mismatches, unusual drop rates, or financial outliers — alerting principals and ops teams days or weeks before end-of-period crunch.

matches.match_rationale: The end-to-end reporting workflow — export from SIS, export from LMS, export from finance, consolidate, reconcile, format — is a multi-step, multi-system process with defined branching logic (flag mismatches, escalate to finance, regenerate after corrections). This is a canonical task automation agent use case. The 5-7 day manual cycle maps directly to a workflow that an AI agent could execute autonomously on a scheduled trigger.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A scheduled reporting agent that pulls exports from connected systems, runs reconciliation checks, assembles the consolidated report, flags exceptions for human review, and delivers a draft report to principals within hours of period close — compressing 5-7 days to same-day.

matches.match_rationale: Campus principals currently wait 2-3 weeks to receive performance narratives. Once the data is consolidated, an LLM can automatically generate the written board narrative, principal summary, and period commentary from structured inputs — turning raw enrollment and performance tables into readable management reports. This is a high-value, low-risk layer on top of the data assembly pipeline.
matches.opportunity_sketch: After data consolidation, an LLM generates a plain-language monthly summary for each campus principal (enrollment trends, drop rate commentary, academic performance highlights) — delivered automatically within 24 hours of period close rather than 2-3 weeks.

matches.match_rationale: The pain point explicitly names the absence of historical trend analysis and the inability to see enrollment or drop-rate trends in a timely way. Time series forecasting directly addresses this — modeling enrollment trajectories, projected drop rates, and academic performance trends across periods, enabling forward-looking visibility rather than backward-looking reconciliation.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A forecasting module that ingests historical SIS/LMS data to project end-of-term enrollment, expected drop rates, and academic performance by campus — giving principals 4-6 week forward visibility instead of 2-3 week lag reports.
